name: Chocolate-Nut Fingers
recipeIngredient: 2 cups pecans or walnuts, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. About 3 hours before serving or early in the day, line a 12-inch by 8-inch baking pan with waxed paper.
2. In a large bowl, mix nuts and rice cereal.
3. In a heavy 2-quart saucepan over low heat, melt chocolate squares, milk, and butter, stirring frequently until smooth. Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla extract. Pour the chocolate mixture over the nut mixture; stir to combine well. Press the nut mixture evenly into the bottom of the pan. Refrigerate for about 1 hour to set the chocolate.
4. Prepare the Chocolate Glaze by melting chocolate, butter, corn syrup, and salt in a 1-quart saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly until smooth.
5. Remove the chocolate-walnut loaf from the pan and waxed paper. Pour the Chocolate Glaze over the loaf. Using a metal spatula, spread the glaze evenly over the loaf. Refrigerate until the glaze is set, about 1 hour.
6. Cut the loaf lengthwise into 2 strips. Cut each strip crosswise into 18 bars. Makes 36 bars, approximately 105 calories each.
